expectations.text: Looks for relevant patterns in the codebase or skill instructions instead of inventing the design in isolation.
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly states 'No target repository was provided. Findings are based on conventional Python CLI project layout' and names specific conventional files (cli.py, config.py, tests/, pyproject.toml) with their roles — demonstrating pattern-grounded reasoning rather than arbitrary invention.

expectations.text: Creates a concrete file-backed handoff artifact at a path matching the workflow's handoff contract.
expectations.evidence: Artifact written to outputs/.agents/scratchpad/cli-config-validation/handoff-plan.md; full absolute path provided in Artifact Status section. File exists on disk.

expectations.text: The artifact content follows a reusable template shape and includes implementation slices, file-level map, and validation plan.
expectations.evidence: Artifact contains ## Implementation Slices (3 slices), ## File-by-File Implementation Map (with full code for each file), and ## Validation Plan with automated and manual steps.

expectations.text: Artifact metadata fields are concretely filled (no placeholder bracket values) for status, track, and next step.
expectations.evidence: Status: 'ready for implementation'; Track: 'Standard'; Recommended Next Step: 'implement directly — all blocking questions resolved via stated assumptions; no codebase exploration was possible...' — all concrete, no bracket placeholders.

expectations.text: Each referenced file in Relevant Findings includes why that file matters.
expectations.evidence: All four entries in Relevant Findings include reasons: cli.py ('CLI entry point — main()/cli() is where validate_config() must be called first'), config.py ('May already hold env-var reads; consolidate there if...'), tests/ ('Add test_config_validator.py alongside existing test files'), pyproject.toml ('Confirm no third-party config library is already in use').

expectations.text: Open questions are tightly bounded to truly blocking items and include risk framing.
expectations.evidence: Response states 'Open Questions: None blocking. All resolved via stated assumptions.' Artifact lists 4 assumptions each with explicit 'Risk if wrong:' framing, plus one non-blocking open question with rationale. Tight and risk-framed throughout.

expectations.text: Provides artifact status in the response with the exact artifact path.
expectations.evidence: Artifact Status section in the response states path as both relative ('outputs/.agents/scratchpad/cli-config-validation/handoff-plan.md') and full absolute path.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.
expectations.evidence: Response includes 'Why this design: stdlib-only (no new dependencies), fully unit-testable via monkeypatch...' and artifact has 'Why this over alternatives:' comparing inline-in-main, extend config.py, and pydantic-settings options.

expectations.text: The Validation Plan section of the handoff artifact contains TDD completion, a code-simplifier refactor pass, and independent code-reviewer review as named discrete steps — not buried solely in the Implementation Slices section.
expectations.evidence: The ## Validation Plan section contains only: pytest command, manual python -m commands, and a 'Review focus' covering empty-string handling and call-site verification. TDD completion, code-simplifier refactor pass, and code-reviewer review do not appear as named discrete steps anywhere in the Validation Plan.
